elements.sent-2411-001: A graph $G$ is $(c,t)$-sparse if for every pair of vertex subsets $A,B\subset V(G)$ with $|A|,|B|\geq t$, $e(A,B)\leq (1-c)|A||B|$.
elements.sent-2411-002: In this paper we prove that for every $c>0$ and integer $\ell$, there exists $C>1$ such that if an $n$-vertex graph $G$ is $(c,t)$-sparse for some $t$, and has at least $C t^{1-1/\ell n^{1+1/\ell}$ edges, then $G$ contains an induced copy of $C_{2\ell}$.}
elements.sent-2411-003: This resolves a conjecture of Fox, Nenadov and Pham.
elements.sent-2411-004: For a graph $H$, its Tur{\'an number , denoted by $\ex(n,H)$, is the maximum number of edges in an $n$-vertex graph which does not contain a copy of $H$ as a subgraph.}
elements.sent-2411-005: The Tur{\'a n type problem is one of the most fundamental topics in extremal combinatorics, with its origin dating back to Mantel in 1907, who solved the case when forbidding triangles.}
elements.sent-2411-006: This was later generalised to all cliques by Tur{\'a n in 1941.}
elements.sent-2411-007: More generally, the celebrated Erd\H{o s-Stone-Simonovits theorem \cite{erdos1966limit,erdos1946structure states that $\ex(n,H)=\left(1-\frac{1 {\chi(H)-1 +o(1)\right)\binom{n {2}$, where $\chi(H)$ is the chromatic number of $H$.}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-008: This theorem asymptotically solves the Tur{\'a n problem for non-bipartite graphs.}
elements.sent-2411-009: However, when $H$ is bipartite, our knowledge about $\ex(n,H)$ is rather limited.
elements.sent-2411-010: Even for complete bipartite graphs and even cycles, the determination of the order of the magnitudes of their Tur{\'a n numbers turns out to be notoriously difficult.}
elements.sent-2411-011: Let $C_{2\ell}$ be the cycle of length $2\ell$.

elements.sent-2411-274: The following claim completes the proof of~\Cref{lem: main .}
elements.sent-2411-275: There exist $P_1\in \C P_{\varphi(z_1)}$ and $P_2\in \C P_{\varphi(z_m)}$ such that $P_1\cup P_2\cup \varphi(P)$ is an induced $C_{2\ell}$ in $G$.
elements.sent-2411-276: Then for any $s$-path $P'\in \C P'_{\varphi(z)}$, $P'\cup \varphi(P)$ is an induced $(2\ell-s)$-path in $G$.
elements.sent-2411-277: By $(b)$ and $(c)$ in~\Cref{lm:2l-2s , the number of $s$-paths removed from $\C P_{\varphi(z)}$ is at most $(m-1)\varepsilon' \gamma t+ (m-1)\le \frac{\gamma t {2}$, and thus $|\C P'_{\varphi(z) |\ge \gamma t/2$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-278: We next show that there exist $P_1\in \C P'_{\varphi(z_1)}$ and $P_2\in \C P'_{\varphi(z_m)}$ such that no edge in $G$ connects their internal sets (which also implies that their internal sets are disjoint), and then $P_1\cup P_2\cup \varphi(P)$ is an induced $C_{2\ell}$ as desired.
elements.sent-2411-279: Let $V_1$ and $V_2$ be the collection of all the internal vertices of paths in $\C P'_{\varphi(z_1)}$ and $\C P'_{\varphi(z_m)}$ respectively.
elements.sent-2411-280: Then $|V_1| = (s-1)|\C P'_{\varphi(z_1) | \ge (s-1) \frac{\gamma t {2 \ge \beta t$, and $|V_2|= (s-1)|\C P'_{\varphi(z_m) | \ge \beta t$.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-281: Suppose to contrary that for any $P_1\in \C P'_{\varphi(z_1)}$ and $P_2\in \C P'_{\varphi(z_m)}$, there exists an edge connecting their internal sets.
elements.sent-2411-282: Then we have \begin{equation}e(V_1,V_2)\ge |\C P'_{\varphi(z_1)}| \cdot |\C P'_{\varphi(z_m)}| = \frac{1}{(s-1)^2}|V_1||V_2| >\varepsilon |V_1||V_2|,\end{equation} which contradicts the $(1-\varepsilon, \beta t)$-sparseness of $G$.. \end{poc.}
elements.sent-2411-283: In this paper, we proved that an induced analogue of Bondy-Simonovits theorem that every $(c,t)$-sparse graph $G$ with at least $C_{c,\ell t^{1-1/\ell n^{1+1/\ell}$ edges contains an induced copy of $C_{2\ell}$.}}
elements.sent-2411-284: It would be interesting to establish, if true, the corresponding supersaturation result.
